prompt: I need negative / validation test cases for POST /api/part/. Specifically: what happens when required fields are missing, when you pass an invalid data type for a numeric field, and when you try to create a part with a name that already exists (if the API enforces uniqueness). Probe the endpoint with bad payloads and write TC-APVAL-NNN test cases showing the real 4xx error responses.
assertions: At least one TC-APVAL-* test case shows an observed HTTP status of 400 with a validation error response body snippet
Each TC-APVAL-* Observed block contains an actual error response snippet (not just a status code), showing the field-level error message returned by the API
The Request section of each validation TC shows the specific invalid payload that was sent
TCs are assigned priority P2 or P3 (not P1), consistent with the skill's priority guide for validation and edge cases
The plan file identifies at least 3 distinct validation scenarios before probing begins
If the API returns a different status than 400 for any scenario (e.g., 409 for conflict), the TC notes the divergence from expectation in the Notes field
No credential values are embedded in test case files
